You are an LLVM compiler expert specializing in test case generation and bug detection. You are conducting LLVM code review now.

## Context
Maintainers provided a patch, but patches often have edge cases or introduce new issues.

## Your Task
You will work in two phases:
- Phase 1: Analyze the fix to identify potential issues, gaps, and edge cases. Propose test strategies.
- Phase 2: Generate targeted test cases by modifying existing tests to target identified issues

## You Will Receive
- Original patch description and related test case(s)
- The code region that was modified (the patch)
- Information about the optimization pass and component involved

## Tools You Can Use

You have multiple tools for each phase, but you should try to avoid using them with same inputs repeatedly to reduce redundant calls.

**CRITICAL**: You MUST use tool call every action in Phase 1 and Phase 2.

------------------------------------------------------------
 36 LLVM_ABI_FOR_TEST extern cl::opt<bool> VerifyEachVPlan;
 37 LLVM_ABI_FOR_TEST extern cl::opt<bool> EnableWideActiveLaneMask;
 38 
 39 #if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
 40 LLVM_ABI_FOR_TEST extern cl::opt<bool> PrintAfterEachVPlanPass;
 41 #endif
 42 
 43 struct VPlanTransforms {
 44   /// Helper to run a VPlan pass \p Pass on \p VPlan, forwarding extra arguments
 45   /// to the pass. Performs verification/printing after each VPlan pass if
 46   /// requested via command line options.
 47   template <bool EnableVerify = true, typename PassTy, typename... ArgsTy>
 48   static decltype(auto) runPass(StringRef PassName, PassTy &&Pass, VPlan &Plan,
 49                                 ArgsTy &&...Args) {
 50     scope_exit PostTransformActions{[&]() {
 51 #if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
 52       // Make sure to print before verification, so that output is more useful
 53       // in case of failures:
 54       if (PrintAfterEachVPlanPass) {
 55         dbgs() << "VPlan after " << PassName << '\n';
 56         dbgs() << Plan << '\n';
 57       }
 58 #endif
 59       if (VerifyEachVPlan && EnableVerify)
 60         verifyVPlanIsValid(Plan);
 61     }};
 62 
 63     return std::forward<PassTy>(Pass)(Plan, std::forward<ArgsTy>(Args)...);
 64   }
 65 #define RUN_VPLAN_PASS(PASS, ...)                                              \
 66   llvm::VPlanTransforms::runPass(#PASS, PASS, __VA_ARGS__)
 67 #define RUN_VPLAN_PASS_NO_VERIFY(PASS, ...)                                    \
 68   llvm::VPlanTransforms::runPass<false>(#PASS, PASS, __VA_ARGS__)
 69 
 70   /// Create a base VPlan0, serving as the common starting point for all later
 71   /// candidates. It consists of an initial plain CFG loop with loop blocks from
 72   /// \p TheLoop being directly translated to VPBasicBlocks with VPInstruction
 73   /// corresponding to the input IR.
 74   ///
 75   /// The created loop is wrapped in an initial skeleton to facilitate
 76   /// vectorization, consisting of a vector pre-header, an exit block for the
 77   /// main vector loop (middle.block) and a new block as preheader of the scalar
 78   /// loop (scalar.ph). See below for an illustration. It also adds a canonical
 79   /// IV and its increment, using \p InductionTy and \p IVDL, and creates a
 80   /// VPValue expression for the original trip count.
 81   ///
 82   ///    [ ] <-- Plan's entry VPIRBasicBlock, wrapping the original loop's
 83   ///    / \       old preheader. Will contain iteration number check and SCEV
 84   ///   |   |      expansions.
 85   ///   |   |
 86   ///   /   v
 87   ///  |   [ ] <-- vector loop bypass (may consist of multiple blocks) will be
 88   ///  |  / |      added later.
 89   ///  | /  v
 90   ///  ||  [ ]     <-- vector pre header.
 91   ///  |/   |
 92   ///  |    v
 93   ///  |   [  ] \  <-- plain CFG loop wrapping original loop to be vectorized.
 94   ///  |   [  ]_|
 95   ///  |    |
 96   ///  |    v
 97   ///  |   [ ]   <--- middle-block with the branch to successors
 98   ///  |   / |
 99   ///  |  /  |
100   ///  | |   v
101   ///  \--->[ ]   <--- scalar preheader (initial a VPBasicBlock, which will be
102   ///    |   |        replaced later by a VPIRBasicBlock wrapping the scalar
103   ///    |   |         preheader basic block.
104   ///    |   |
105   ///        v      <-- edge from middle to exit iff epilogue is not required.
106   ///    |  [ ] \
107   ///    |  [ ]_|   <-- old scalar loop to handle remainder (scalar epilogue,
108   ///    |   |          header wrapped in VPIRBasicBlock).
109   ///    \   |
110   ///     \  v
111   ///      >[ ]     <-- original loop exit block(s), wrapped in VPIRBasicBlocks.
112   LLVM_ABI_FOR_TEST static std::unique_ptr<VPlan>
113   buildVPlan0(Loop *TheLoop, LoopInfo &LI, Type *InductionTy, DebugLoc IVDL,
114               PredicatedScalarEvolution &PSE, LoopVersioning *LVer = nullptr);
115 
116   /// Replace VPPhi recipes in \p Plan's header with corresponding
117   /// VPHeaderPHIRecipe subclasses for inductions, reductions, and
118   /// fixed-order recurrences. This processes all header phis and creates
119   /// the appropriate widened recipe for each one.
120   static void createHeaderPhiRecipes(
121       VPlan &Plan, PredicatedScalarEvolution &PSE, Loop &OrigLoop,
122       const MapVector<PHINode *, InductionDescriptor> &Inductions,
123       const MapVector<PHINode *, RecurrenceDescriptor> &Reductions,
124       const SmallPtrSetImpl<const PHINode *> &FixedOrderRecurrences,
125       const SmallPtrSetImpl<PHINode *> &InLoopReductions, bool AllowReordering);
126 
127   /// Create VPReductionRecipes for in-loop reductions. This processes chains
128   /// of operations contributing to in-loop reductions and creates appropriate
129   /// VPReductionRecipe instances. Block masks from \p BlockMaskCache are used
130   /// to add predication for blocks in \p BlocksNeedingPredication.
131   static void createInLoopReductionRecipes(
132       VPlan &Plan, const DenseMap<VPBasicBlock *, VPValue *> &BlockMaskCache,
133       const DenseSet<BasicBlock *> &BlocksNeedingPredication,
134       ElementCount MinVF);
135 
136   /// Update \p Plan to account for all early exits.
137   LLVM_ABI_FOR_TEST static void handleEarlyExits(VPlan &Plan,
138                                                  bool HasUncountableExit);
139 
140   /// If a check is needed to guard executing the scalar epilogue loop, it will
141   /// be added to the middle block.
142   LLVM_ABI_FOR_TEST static void addMiddleCheck(VPlan &Plan,
143                                                bool RequiresScalarEpilogueCheck,
144                                                bool TailFolded);
145 
146   // Create a check to \p Plan to see if the vector loop should be executed.
147   static void
148   addMinimumIterationCheck(VPlan &Plan, ElementCount VF, unsigned UF,
149                            ElementCount MinProfitableTripCount,
150                            bool RequiresScalarEpilogue, bool TailFolded,
151                            bool CheckNeededWithTailFolding, Loop *OrigLoop,
152                            const uint32_t *MinItersBypassWeights, DebugLoc DL,
153                            PredicatedScalarEvolution &PSE);
154 
155   /// Add a check to \p Plan to see if the epilogue vector loop should be
156   /// executed.
157   static void addMinimumVectorEpilogueIterationCheck(
158       VPlan &Plan, Value *TripCount, Value *VectorTripCount,
159       bool RequiresScalarEpilogue, ElementCount EpilogueVF, unsigned EpilogueUF,
160       unsigned MainLoopStep, unsigned EpilogueLoopStep, ScalarEvolution &SE);
161 
162   /// Replace loops in \p Plan's flat CFG with VPRegionBlocks, turning \p Plan's
163   /// flat CFG into a hierarchical CFG.
164   LLVM_ABI_FOR_TEST static void createLoopRegions(VPlan &Plan);
165 
166   /// Wrap runtime check block \p CheckBlock in a VPIRBB and \p Cond in a
167   /// VPValue and connect the block to \p Plan, using the VPValue as branch
168   /// condition.
169   static void attachCheckBlock(VPlan &Plan, Value *Cond, BasicBlock *CheckBlock,
170                                bool AddBranchWeights);
171 
172   /// Replaces the VPInstructions in \p Plan with corresponding
173   /// widen recipes. Returns false if any VPInstructions could not be converted
174   /// to a wide recipe if needed.
175   LLVM_ABI_FOR_TEST static bool
176   tryToConvertVPInstructionsToVPRecipes(VPlan &Plan,
177                                         const TargetLibraryInfo &TLI);
178 
179   /// Try to legalize reductions with multiple in-loop uses. Currently only
180   /// min/max reductions used by FindLastIV reductions are supported. Otherwise
181   /// return false.
182   static bool handleMultiUseReductions(VPlan &Plan);
183 
184   /// Try to have all users of fixed-order recurrences appear after the recipe
185   /// defining their previous value, by either sinking users or hoisting recipes
186   /// defining their previous value (and its operands). Then introduce
187   /// FirstOrderRecurrenceSplice VPInstructions to combine the value from the
188   /// recurrence phis and previous values.
189   /// \returns true if all users of fixed-order recurrences could be re-arranged
190   /// as needed or false if it is not possible. In the latter case, \p Plan is
191   /// not valid.
192   static bool adjustFixedOrderRecurrences(VPlan &Plan, VPBuilder &Builder);
193 
194   /// Check if \p Plan contains any FMaxNum or FMinNum reductions. If they do,
195   /// try to update the vector loop to exit early if any input is NaN and resume
196   /// executing in the scalar loop to handle the NaNs there. Return false if
197   /// this attempt was unsuccessful.
198   static bool handleMaxMinNumReductions(VPlan &Plan);
199 
200   /// Check if \p Plan contains any FindLast reductions. If it does, try to
201   /// update the vector loop to save the appropriate state using selects
202   /// for entire vectors for both the latest mask containing at least one active
203   /// element and the corresponding data vector. Return false if this attempt
204   /// was unsuccessful.
205   static bool handleFindLastReductions(VPlan &Plan);
206 
207   /// Clear NSW/NUW flags from reduction instructions if necessary.
208   static void clearReductionWrapFlags(VPlan &Plan);
209 
210   /// Explicitly unroll \p Plan by \p UF.
211   static void unrollByUF(VPlan &Plan, unsigned UF);
212 
213   /// Replace each replicating VPReplicateRecipe and VPInstruction outside of
214   /// any replicate region in \p Plan with \p VF single-scalar recipes.
215   /// TODO: Also replicate VPScalarIVSteps and VPReplicateRecipes inside
216   /// replicate regions, thereby dissolving the latter.
217   static void replicateByVF(VPlan &Plan, ElementCount VF);
218 
219   /// Optimize \p Plan based on \p BestVF and \p BestUF. This may restrict the
220   /// resulting plan to \p BestVF and \p BestUF.
221   static void optimizeForVFAndUF(VPlan &Plan, ElementCount BestVF,
222                                  unsigned BestUF,
223                                  PredicatedScalarEvolution &PSE);
224 
225   /// Apply VPlan-to-VPlan optimizations to \p Plan, including induction recipe
226   /// optimizations, dead recipe removal, replicate region optimizations and
227   /// block merging.
228   LLVM_ABI_FOR_TEST static void optimize(VPlan &Plan);
229 
230   /// Wrap predicated VPReplicateRecipes with a mask operand in an if-then
231   /// region block and remove the mask operand. Optimize the created regions by
232   /// iteratively sinking scalar operands into the region, followed by merging
233   /// regions until no improvements are remaining.
234   static void createAndOptimizeReplicateRegions(VPlan &Plan);
235 
236   /// Replace (ICMP_ULE, wide canonical IV, backedge-taken-count) checks with an
237   /// (active-lane-mask recipe, wide canonical IV, trip-count). If \p
238   /// UseActiveLaneMaskForControlFlow is true, introduce an
239   /// VPActiveLaneMaskPHIRecipe. If \p DataAndControlFlowWithoutRuntimeCheck is
240   /// true, no minimum-iteration runtime check will be created (during skeleton
241   /// creation) and instead it is handled using active-lane-mask. \p
242   /// DataAndControlFlowWithoutRuntimeCheck implies \p
243   /// UseActiveLaneMaskForControlFlow.
244   static void addActiveLaneMask(VPlan &Plan,
245                                 bool UseActiveLaneMaskForControlFlow,
246                                 bool DataAndControlFlowWithoutRuntimeCheck);
247 
248   /// Insert truncates and extends for any truncated recipe. Redundant casts
249   /// will be folded later.
250   static void
251   truncateToMinimalBitwidths(VPlan &Plan,
252                              const MapVector<Instruction *, uint64_t> &MinBWs);
253 
254   /// Replace symbolic strides from \p StridesMap in \p Plan with constants when
255   /// possible.
256   static void
257   replaceSymbolicStrides(VPlan &Plan, PredicatedScalarEvolution &PSE,
258                          const DenseMap<Value *, const SCEV *> &StridesMap);
259 
260   /// Drop poison flags from recipes that may generate a poison value that is
261   /// used after vectorization, even when their operands are not poison. Those
262   /// recipes meet the following conditions:
263   ///  * Contribute to the address computation of a recipe generating a widen
264   ///    memory load/store (VPWidenMemoryInstructionRecipe or
265   ///    VPInterleaveRecipe).
266   ///  * Such a widen memory load/store has at least one underlying Instruction
267   ///    that is in a basic block that needs predication and after vectorization
268   ///    the generated instruction won't be predicated.
269   /// Uses \p BlockNeedsPredication to check if a block needs predicating.
270   /// TODO: Replace BlockNeedsPredication callback with retrieving info from
271   ///       VPlan directly.
272   static void dropPoisonGeneratingRecipes(
273       VPlan &Plan,
274       const std::function<bool(BasicBlock *)> &BlockNeedsPredication);
275 
276   /// Add a VPEVLBasedIVPHIRecipe and related recipes to \p Plan and
277   /// replaces all uses except the canonical IV increment of
278   /// VPCanonicalIVPHIRecipe with a VPEVLBasedIVPHIRecipe.
279   /// VPCanonicalIVPHIRecipe is only used to control the loop after
280   /// this transformation.
281   static void
282   addExplicitVectorLength(VPlan &Plan,
283                           const std::optional<unsigned> &MaxEVLSafeElements);
284 
285   /// Optimize recipes which use an EVL-based header mask to VP intrinsics, for
------------------------------------------------------------

286   /// example:
287   ///
288   /// %mask = icmp ult step-vector, EVL
289   /// %load = load %ptr, %mask
290   /// -->
291   /// %load = vp.load %ptr, EVL
292   static void optimizeEVLMasks(VPlan &Plan);
293 
294   // For each Interleave Group in \p InterleaveGroups replace the Recipes
295   // widening its memory instructions with a single VPInterleaveRecipe at its
296   // insertion point.
297   static void createInterleaveGroups(
298       VPlan &Plan,
299       const SmallPtrSetImpl<const InterleaveGroup<Instruction> *>
300           &InterleaveGroups,
301       VPRecipeBuilder &RecipeBuilder, const bool &ScalarEpilogueAllowed);
302 
303   /// Remove dead recipes from \p Plan.
304   static void removeDeadRecipes(VPlan &Plan);
305 
306   /// Update \p Plan to account for the uncountable early exit from \p
307   /// EarlyExitingVPBB to \p EarlyExitVPBB by introducing a BranchOnTwoConds
308   /// terminator in the latch that handles the early exit and the latch exit
309   /// condition.
310   static void handleUncountableEarlyExit(VPBasicBlock *EarlyExitingVPBB,
311                                          VPBasicBlock *EarlyExitVPBB,
312                                          VPlan &Plan, VPBasicBlock *HeaderVPBB,
313                                          VPBasicBlock *LatchVPBB);
314 
315   /// Replace loop regions with explicit CFG.
316   static void dissolveLoopRegions(VPlan &Plan);
317 
318   /// Expand BranchOnTwoConds instructions into explicit CFG with
319   /// BranchOnCond instructions. Should be called after dissolveLoopRegions.
320   static void expandBranchOnTwoConds(VPlan &Plan);
321 
322   /// Transform EVL loops to use variable-length stepping after region
323   /// dissolution.
324   ///
325   /// Once loop regions are replaced with explicit CFG, EVL loops can step with
326   /// variable vector lengths instead of fixed lengths. This transformation:
327   ///  * Makes EVL-Phi concrete.
328   //   * Removes CanonicalIV and increment.
329   ///  * Replaces the exit condition from
330   ///      (branch-on-count CanonicalIVInc, VectorTripCount)
331   ///    to
332   ///      (branch-on-cond eq AVLNext, 0)
333   static void canonicalizeEVLLoops(VPlan &Plan);
334 
335   /// Lower abstract recipes to concrete ones, that can be codegen'd.
336   static void convertToConcreteRecipes(VPlan &Plan);
337 
338   /// This function converts initial recipes to the abstract recipes and clamps
339   /// \p Range based on cost model for following optimizations and cost
340   /// estimations. The converted abstract recipes will lower to concrete
341   /// recipes before codegen.
342   static void convertToAbstractRecipes(VPlan &Plan, VPCostContext &Ctx,
343                                        VFRange &Range);
344 
345   /// Perform instcombine-like simplifications on recipes in \p Plan.
346   static void simplifyRecipes(VPlan &Plan);
347 
348   /// Remove BranchOnCond recipes with true or false conditions together with
349   /// removing dead edges to their successors.
350   static void removeBranchOnConst(VPlan &Plan);
351 
352   /// Perform common-subexpression-elimination on \p Plan.
353   static void cse(VPlan &Plan);
354 
355   /// If there's a single exit block, optimize its phi recipes that use exiting
356   /// IV values by feeding them precomputed end values instead, possibly taken
357   /// one step backwards.
358   static void
359   optimizeInductionExitUsers(VPlan &Plan,
360                              DenseMap<VPValue *, VPValue *> &EndValues,
361                              PredicatedScalarEvolution &PSE);
362 
363   /// Add explicit broadcasts for live-ins and VPValues defined in \p Plan's entry block if they are used as vectors.
364   static void materializeBroadcasts(VPlan &Plan);
365 
366   /// Hoist single-scalar loads with invariant addresses out of the vector loop
367   /// to the preheader, if they are proven not to alias with any stores in the
368   /// plan using noalias metadata.
369   static void hoistInvariantLoads(VPlan &Plan);
370 
371   /// Hoist predicated loads from the same address to the loop entry block, if
372   /// they are guaranteed to execute on both paths (i.e., in replicate regions
373   /// with complementary masks P and NOT P).
374   static void hoistPredicatedLoads(VPlan &Plan, PredicatedScalarEvolution &PSE,
375                                    const Loop *L);
376 
377   /// Sink predicated stores to the same address with complementary predicates
378   /// (P and NOT P) to an unconditional store with select recipes for the
379   /// stored values. This eliminates branching overhead when all paths
380   /// unconditionally store to the same location.
381   static void sinkPredicatedStores(VPlan &Plan, PredicatedScalarEvolution &PSE,
382                                    const Loop *L);
383 
384   // Materialize vector trip counts for constants early if it can simply be
385   // computed as (Original TC / VF * UF) * VF * UF.
386   static void
387   materializeConstantVectorTripCount(VPlan &Plan, ElementCount BestVF,
388                                      unsigned BestUF,
389                                      PredicatedScalarEvolution &PSE);
390 
391   /// Materialize vector trip count computations to a set of VPInstructions.
392   static void materializeVectorTripCount(VPlan &Plan,
393                                          VPBasicBlock *VectorPHVPBB,
394                                          bool TailByMasking,
395                                          bool RequiresScalarEpilogue);
396 
397   /// Materialize the backedge-taken count to be computed explicitly using
398   /// VPInstructions.
399   static void materializeBackedgeTakenCount(VPlan &Plan,
400                                             VPBasicBlock *VectorPH);
401 
402   /// Add explicit Build[Struct]Vector recipes to Pack multiple scalar values
403   /// into vectors and Unpack recipes to extract scalars from vectors as
404   /// needed.
405   static void materializePacksAndUnpacks(VPlan &Plan);
406 
407   /// Materialize VF and VFxUF to be computed explicitly using VPInstructions.
408   static void materializeVFAndVFxUF(VPlan &Plan, VPBasicBlock *VectorPH,
409                                     ElementCount VF);
410 
411   /// Expand VPExpandSCEVRecipes in \p Plan's entry block. Each
412   /// VPExpandSCEVRecipe is replaced with a live-in wrapping the expanded IR
413   /// value. A mapping from SCEV expressions to their expanded IR value is
414   /// returned.
415   static DenseMap<const SCEV *, Value *> expandSCEVs(VPlan &Plan,
416                                                      ScalarEvolution &SE);
417 
418   /// Try to convert a plan with interleave groups with VF elements to a plan
419   /// with the interleave groups replaced by wide loads and stores processing VF
420   /// elements, if all transformed interleave groups access the full vector
421   /// width (checked via \o VectorRegWidth). This effectively is a very simple
422   /// form of loop-aware SLP, where we use interleave groups to identify
423   /// candidates.
424   static void narrowInterleaveGroups(VPlan &Plan, ElementCount VF,
425                                      TypeSize VectorRegWidth);
426 
427   /// Predicate and linearize the control-flow in the only loop region of
428   /// \p Plan. If \p FoldTail is true, create a mask guarding the loop
429   /// header, otherwise use all-true for the header mask. Masks for blocks are
430   /// added to a block-to-mask map which is returned in order to be used later
431   /// for wide recipe construction. This argument is temporary and will be
432   /// removed in the future.
433   static DenseMap<VPBasicBlock *, VPValue *>
434   introduceMasksAndLinearize(VPlan &Plan, bool FoldTail);
435 
436   /// Add branch weight metadata, if the \p Plan's middle block is terminated by
437   /// a BranchOnCond recipe.
438   static void
439   addBranchWeightToMiddleTerminator(VPlan &Plan, ElementCount VF,
440                                     std::optional<unsigned> VScaleForTuning);
441 
442   /// Update the resume phis in the scalar preheader after creating wide recipes
443   /// for first-order recurrences, reductions and inductions. End values for
444   /// inductions are added to \p IVEndValues.
445   static void
446   updateScalarResumePhis(VPlan &Plan,
447                          DenseMap<VPValue *, VPValue *> &IVEndValues);
448 
449   /// Handle users in the exit block for first order reductions in the original
450   /// exit block. The penultimate value of recurrences is fed to their LCSSA phi
451   /// users in the original exit block using the VPIRInstruction wrapping to the
452   /// LCSSA phi.
453   static void addExitUsersForFirstOrderRecurrences(VPlan &Plan, VFRange &Range);
454 };
455 
456 } // namespace llvm
457 
458 #endif // LLVM_TRANSFORMS_VECTORIZE_VPLANTRANSFORMS_H

file: llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlanTransforms.h:40-290
------------------------------------------------------------
 40 LLVM_ABI_FOR_TEST extern cl::opt<bool> PrintAfterEachVPlanPass;
 41 #endif
 42 
 43 struct VPlanTransforms {
 44   /// Helper to run a VPlan pass \p Pass on \p VPlan, forwarding extra arguments
 45   /// to the pass. Performs verification/printing after each VPlan pass if
 46   /// requested via command line options.
 47   template <bool EnableVerify = true, typename PassTy, typename... ArgsTy>
 48   static decltype(auto) runPass(StringRef PassName, PassTy &&Pass, VPlan &Plan,
 49                                 ArgsTy &&...Args) {
 50     scope_exit PostTransformActions{[&]() {
 51 #if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
 52       // Make sure to print before verification, so that output is more useful
 53       // in case of failures:
 54       if (PrintAfterEachVPlanPass) {
 55         dbgs() << "VPlan after " << PassName << '\n';
 56         dbgs() << Plan << '\n';
 57       }
 58 #endif
 59       if (VerifyEachVPlan && EnableVerify)
 60         verifyVPlanIsValid(Plan);
 61     }};
 62 
 63     return std::forward<PassTy>(Pass)(Plan, std::forward<ArgsTy>(Args)...);
 64   }
 65 #define RUN_VPLAN_PASS(PASS, ...)                                              \
 66   llvm::VPlanTransforms::runPass(#PASS, PASS, __VA_ARGS__)
 67 #define RUN_VPLAN_PASS_NO_VERIFY(PASS, ...)                                    \
 68   llvm::VPlanTransforms::runPass<false>(#PASS, PASS, __VA_ARGS__)
 69 
 70   /// Create a base VPlan0, serving as the common starting point for all later
 71   /// candidates. It consists of an initial plain CFG loop with loop blocks from
 72   /// \p TheLoop being directly translated to VPBasicBlocks with VPInstruction
 73   /// corresponding to the input IR.
 74   ///
 75   /// The created loop is wrapped in an initial skeleton to facilitate
 76   /// vectorization, consisting of a vector pre-header, an exit block for the
 77   /// main vector loop (middle.block) and a new block as preheader of the scalar
 78   /// loop (scalar.ph). See below for an illustration. It also adds a canonical
 79   /// IV and its increment, using \p InductionTy and \p IVDL, and creates a
 80   /// VPValue expression for the original trip count.
 81   ///
 82   ///    [ ] <-- Plan's entry VPIRBasicBlock, wrapping the original loop's
 83   ///    / \       old preheader. Will contain iteration number check and SCEV
 84   ///   |   |      expansions.
 85   ///   |   |
 86   ///   /   v
 87   ///  |   [ ] <-- vector loop bypass (may consist of multiple blocks) will be
 88   ///  |  / |      added later.
 89   ///  | /  v
 90   ///  ||  [ ]     <-- vector pre header.
 91   ///  |/   |
 92   ///  |    v
 93   ///  |   [  ] \  <-- plain CFG loop wrapping original loop to be vectorized.
 94   ///  |   [  ]_|
 95   ///  |    |
 96   ///  |    v
 97   ///  |   [ ]   <--- middle-block with the branch to successors
 98   ///  |   / |
 99   ///  |  /  |
100   ///  | |   v
101   ///  \--->[ ]   <--- scalar preheader (initial a VPBasicBlock, which will be
102   ///    |   |        replaced later by a VPIRBasicBlock wrapping the scalar
103   ///    |   |         preheader basic block.
104   ///    |   |
105   ///        v      <-- edge from middle to exit iff epilogue is not required.
106   ///    |  [ ] \
107   ///    |  [ ]_|   <-- old scalar loop to handle remainder (scalar epilogue,
108   ///    |   |          header wrapped in VPIRBasicBlock).
109   ///    \   |
110   ///     \  v
111   ///      >[ ]     <-- original loop exit block(s), wrapped in VPIRBasicBlocks.
112   LLVM_ABI_FOR_TEST static std::unique_ptr<VPlan>
113   buildVPlan0(Loop *TheLoop, LoopInfo &LI, Type *InductionTy, DebugLoc IVDL,
114               PredicatedScalarEvolution &PSE, LoopVersioning *LVer = nullptr);
115 
116   /// Replace VPPhi recipes in \p Plan's header with corresponding
117   /// VPHeaderPHIRecipe subclasses for inductions, reductions, and
118   /// fixed-order recurrences. This processes all header phis and creates
119   /// the appropriate widened recipe for each one.
120   static void createHeaderPhiRecipes(
121       VPlan &Plan, PredicatedScalarEvolution &PSE, Loop &OrigLoop,
122       const MapVector<PHINode *, InductionDescriptor> &Inductions,
123       const MapVector<PHINode *, RecurrenceDescriptor> &Reductions,
124       const SmallPtrSetImpl<const PHINode *> &FixedOrderRecurrences,
125       const SmallPtrSetImpl<PHINode *> &InLoopReductions, bool AllowReordering);
126 
127   /// Create VPReductionRecipes for in-loop reductions. This processes chains
128   /// of operations contributing to in-loop reductions and creates appropriate
129   /// VPReductionRecipe instances. Block masks from \p BlockMaskCache are used
130   /// to add predication for blocks in \p BlocksNeedingPredication.
131   static void createInLoopReductionRecipes(
132       VPlan &Plan, const DenseMap<VPBasicBlock *, VPValue *> &BlockMaskCache,
133       const DenseSet<BasicBlock *> &BlocksNeedingPredication,
134       ElementCount MinVF);
135 
136   /// Update \p Plan to account for all early exits.
137   LLVM_ABI_FOR_TEST static void handleEarlyExits(VPlan &Plan,
138                                                  bool HasUncountableExit);
139 
140   /// If a check is needed to guard executing the scalar epilogue loop, it will
141   /// be added to the middle block.
142   LLVM_ABI_FOR_TEST static void addMiddleCheck(VPlan &Plan,
143                                                bool RequiresScalarEpilogueCheck,
144                                                bool TailFolded);
145 
146   // Create a check to \p Plan to see if the vector loop should be executed.
147   static void
148   addMinimumIterationCheck(VPlan &Plan, ElementCount VF, unsigned UF,
149                            ElementCount MinProfitableTripCount,
150                            bool RequiresScalarEpilogue, bool TailFolded,
151                            bool CheckNeededWithTailFolding, Loop *OrigLoop,
152                            const uint32_t *MinItersBypassWeights, DebugLoc DL,
153                            PredicatedScalarEvolution &PSE);
154 
155   /// Add a check to \p Plan to see if the epilogue vector loop should be
156   /// executed.
157   static void addMinimumVectorEpilogueIterationCheck(
158       VPlan &Plan, Value *TripCount, Value *VectorTripCount,
159       bool RequiresScalarEpilogue, ElementCount EpilogueVF, unsigned EpilogueUF,
160       unsigned MainLoopStep, unsigned EpilogueLoopStep, ScalarEvolution &SE);
161 
162   /// Replace loops in \p Plan's flat CFG with VPRegionBlocks, turning \p Plan's
163   /// flat CFG into a hierarchical CFG.
164   LLVM_ABI_FOR_TEST static void createLoopRegions(VPlan &Plan);
165 
166   /// Wrap runtime check block \p CheckBlock in a VPIRBB and \p Cond in a
167   /// VPValue and connect the block to \p Plan, using the VPValue as branch
168   /// condition.
169   static void attachCheckBlock(VPlan &Plan, Value *Cond, BasicBlock *CheckBlock,
170                                bool AddBranchWeights);
171 
172   /// Replaces the VPInstructions in \p Plan with corresponding
173   /// widen recipes. Returns false if any VPInstructions could not be converted
174   /// to a wide recipe if needed.
175   LLVM_ABI_FOR_TEST static bool
176   tryToConvertVPInstructionsToVPRecipes(VPlan &Plan,
177                                         const TargetLibraryInfo &TLI);
178 
179   /// Try to legalize reductions with multiple in-loop uses. Currently only
180   /// min/max reductions used by FindLastIV reductions are supported. Otherwise
181   /// return false.
182   static bool handleMultiUseReductions(VPlan &Plan);
183 
184   /// Try to have all users of fixed-order recurrences appear after the recipe
185   /// defining their previous value, by either sinking users or hoisting recipes
186   /// defining their previous value (and its operands). Then introduce
187   /// FirstOrderRecurrenceSplice VPInstructions to combine the value from the
188   /// recurrence phis and previous values.
189   /// \returns true if all users of fixed-order recurrences could be re-arranged
190   /// as needed or false if it is not possible. In the latter case, \p Plan is
191   /// not valid.
192   static bool adjustFixedOrderRecurrences(VPlan &Plan, VPBuilder &Builder);
193 
194   /// Check if \p Plan contains any FMaxNum or FMinNum reductions. If they do,
195   /// try to update the vector loop to exit early if any input is NaN and resume
196   /// executing in the scalar loop to handle the NaNs there. Return false if
197   /// this attempt was unsuccessful.
198   static bool handleMaxMinNumReductions(VPlan &Plan);
199 
200   /// Check if \p Plan contains any FindLast reductions. If it does, try to
201   /// update the vector loop to save the appropriate state using selects
202   /// for entire vectors for both the latest mask containing at least one active
203   /// element and the corresponding data vector. Return false if this attempt
204   /// was unsuccessful.
205   static bool handleFindLastReductions(VPlan &Plan);
206 
207   /// Clear NSW/NUW flags from reduction instructions if necessary.
208   static void clearReductionWrapFlags(VPlan &Plan);
209 
210   /// Explicitly unroll \p Plan by \p UF.
211   static void unrollByUF(VPlan &Plan, unsigned UF);
212 
213   /// Replace each replicating VPReplicateRecipe and VPInstruction outside of
214   /// any replicate region in \p Plan with \p VF single-scalar recipes.
215   /// TODO: Also replicate VPScalarIVSteps and VPReplicateRecipes inside
216   /// replicate regions, thereby dissolving the latter.
217   static void replicateByVF(VPlan &Plan, ElementCount VF);
218 
219   /// Optimize \p Plan based on \p BestVF and \p BestUF. This may restrict the
220   /// resulting plan to \p BestVF and \p BestUF.
221   static void optimizeForVFAndUF(VPlan &Plan, ElementCount BestVF,
222                                  unsigned BestUF,
223                                  PredicatedScalarEvolution &PSE);
224 
225   /// Apply VPlan-to-VPlan optimizations to \p Plan, including induction recipe
226   /// optimizations, dead recipe removal, replicate region optimizations and
227   /// block merging.
228   LLVM_ABI_FOR_TEST static void optimize(VPlan &Plan);
229 
230   /// Wrap predicated VPReplicateRecipes with a mask operand in an if-then
231   /// region block and remove the mask operand. Optimize the created regions by
232   /// iteratively sinking scalar operands into the region, followed by merging
233   /// regions until no improvements are remaining.
234   static void createAndOptimizeReplicateRegions(VPlan &Plan);
235 
236   /// Replace (ICMP_ULE, wide canonical IV, backedge-taken-count) checks with an
237   /// (active-lane-mask recipe, wide canonical IV, trip-count). If \p
238   /// UseActiveLaneMaskForControlFlow is true, introduce an
239   /// VPActiveLaneMaskPHIRecipe. If \p DataAndControlFlowWithoutRuntimeCheck is
240   /// true, no minimum-iteration runtime check will be created (during skeleton
241   /// creation) and instead it is handled using active-lane-mask. \p
242   /// DataAndControlFlowWithoutRuntimeCheck implies \p
243   /// UseActiveLaneMaskForControlFlow.
244   static void addActiveLaneMask(VPlan &Plan,
245                                 bool UseActiveLaneMaskForControlFlow,
246                                 bool DataAndControlFlowWithoutRuntimeCheck);
247 
248   /// Insert truncates and extends for any truncated recipe. Redundant casts
249   /// will be folded later.
250   static void
251   truncateToMinimalBitwidths(VPlan &Plan,
252                              const MapVector<Instruction *, uint64_t> &MinBWs);
253 
254   /// Replace symbolic strides from \p StridesMap in \p Plan with constants when
255   /// possible.
256   static void
257   replaceSymbolicStrides(VPlan &Plan, PredicatedScalarEvolution &PSE,
258                          const DenseMap<Value *, const SCEV *> &StridesMap);
259 
260   /// Drop poison flags from recipes that may generate a poison value that is
261   /// used after vectorization, even when their operands are not poison. Those
262   /// recipes meet the following conditions:
263   ///  * Contribute to the address computation of a recipe generating a widen
264   ///    memory load/store (VPWidenMemoryInstructionRecipe or
265   ///    VPInterleaveRecipe).
266   ///  * Such a widen memory load/store has at least one underlying Instruction
267   ///    that is in a basic block that needs predication and after vectorization
268   ///    the generated instruction won't be predicated.
269   /// Uses \p BlockNeedsPredication to check if a block needs predicating.
270   /// TODO: Replace BlockNeedsPredication callback with retrieving info from
271   ///       VPlan directly.
272   static void dropPoisonGeneratingRecipes(
273       VPlan &Plan,
274       const std::function<bool(BasicBlock *)> &BlockNeedsPredication);
275 
276   /// Add a VPEVLBasedIVPHIRecipe and related recipes to \p Plan and
277   /// replaces all uses except the canonical IV increment of
278   /// VPCanonicalIVPHIRecipe with a VPEVLBasedIVPHIRecipe.
279   /// VPCanonicalIVPHIRecipe is only used to control the loop after
280   /// this transformation.
281   static void
282   addExplicitVectorLength(VPlan &Plan,
283                           const std::optional<unsigned> &MaxEVLSafeElements);
284

{"strategies":[{"target":"Any existing LoopVectorize test case.","rationale":"The patch adds a new option `-vplan-print-after-all` which prints the VPlan after each pass. We should test if this option works correctly and doesn't cause any crashes or unexpected behavior, especially with different types of loops (e.g., with reductions, inductions, early exits).","name":"Test VPlan printing with `-vplan-print-after-all`","expected_issue":"The compiler might crash or produce incorrect output because the VPlan verification fails or the VPlan is printed incorrectly when `-vplan-print-after-all` is used."},{"target":"Any existing LoopVectorize test case.","rationale":"The patch modifies the `runPass` function to perform verification and printing. We should test if both options work together correctly and don't cause any crashes or unexpected behavior.","name":"Test VPlan printing with `-vplan-print-after-all` and `-vplan-verify-each`","expected_issue":"The compiler might crash or produce incorrect output because the VPlan verification fails or the VPlan is printed incorrectly when `-vplan-print-after-all` is used with `-vplan-verify-each`."},{"target":"Any existing LoopVectorize test case that triggers significant VPlan modifications.","rationale":"The patch modifies the `runPass` function to perform verification and printing. We should test if the printing works correctly even when the VPlan is modified significantly by a pass (e.g., `VPlanTransforms::optimize`).","name":"Test VPlan printing with `-vplan-print-after-all` and a pass that modifies the VPlan significantly","expected_issue":"The compiler might crash or produce incorrect output because the VPlan verification fails or the VPlan is printed incorrectly when `-vplan-print-after-all` is used with a pass that modifies the VPlan significantly."},{"target":"Any existing LoopVectorize test case that triggers a pass that doesn't modify the VPlan.","rationale":"The patch modifies the `runPass` function to perform verification and printing. We should test if the printing works correctly even when the VPlan is not modified by a pass.","name":"Test VPlan printing with `-vplan-print-after-all` and a pass that doesn't modify the VPlan","expected_issue":"The compiler might crash or produce incorrect output because the VPlan verification fails or the VPlan is printed incorrectly when `-vplan-print-after-all` is used with a pass that doesn't modify the VPlan."},{"expected_issue":"The compiler might crash or produce incorrect output because the VPlan verification fails or the VPlan is printed incorrectly when `-vplan-print-after-all` is used with a pass that fails.","target":"Any existing LoopVectorize test case that triggers a pass that fails.","rationale":"The patch modifies the `runPass` function to perform verification and printing. We should test if the printing works correctly even when a pass fails (e.g., returns false).","name":"Test VPlan printing with `-vplan-print-after-all` and a pass that fails"}],"thoughts":"Fix Understanding:
The patch introduces a new hidden command-line option `-vplan-print-after-all` to print the VPlan after each VPlan transform pass. It achieves this by:
1. Adding a `RUN_VPLAN_PASS` macro that wraps the execution of a VPlan pass.
2. Modifying `VPlanTransforms::runPass` to take the pass name and perform printing (if requested) and verification (if requested).
3. Replacing direct calls to `VPlanTransforms::runPass` and some direct pass function calls with `RUN_VPLAN_PASS`.
4. Adding dummy passes `printAfterInitialConstruction` and `printFinalVPlan` to print the initial and final VPlans.

Formal Semantic Modeling:
Before the fix, VPlan passes were executed directly or via `VPlanTransforms::runPass` which only performed verification if `-vplan-verify-each` was set.
After the fix, VPlan passes are executed via `RUN_VPLAN_PASS` which uses `scope_exit` to perform printing (if `-vplan-print-after-all` is set) and verification (if `-vplan-verify-each` is set) *after* the pass executes.

Assumptions Identified:
1. The `RUN_VPLAN_PASS` macro correctly captures the pass name and forwards arguments.
2. The `scope_exit` correctly executes the printing and verification after the pass returns, regardless of whether it returns normally or via an exception (though LLVM doesn't use exceptions, early returns are common).
3. The printing logic (`dbgs() << Plan << '\n';`) is safe to call at any point after a pass.
4. The dummy passes `printAfterInitialConstruction` and `printFinalVPlan` don't affect the VPlan.

Potential Cases to Break Assumptions:
1. If a pass leaves the VPlan in an invalid state temporarily, printing it might crash the compiler. However, the printing is done *before* verification, which is intentional to help debug failures.
2. If a pass returns a value (e.g., `bool`), `RUN_VPLAN_PASS` must correctly return that value. The use of `decltype(auto)` and `std::forward` seems correct.
3. The `RUN_VPLAN_PASS_NO_VERIFY` macro is used for `printAfterInitialConstruction`. This is because the initial VPlan might not pass all verification checks yet.

Test Strategies:
The main goal is to ensure that the new `-vplan-print-after-all` option works correctly and doesn't introduce any regressions or crashes. We should test it with various loop structures and existing options."}

## Your Task for Phase 2

In this phase, you will use the `tests_manager` tool to retrieve existing test cases, apply your proposed mutations, and verify them. You must ensure that **every test case** managed by the `tests_manager` is processed and marked as tested.

Follow this structured workflow:

### Step 1: Retrieve and Prioritize Test Cases
- Use the `tests_manager` tool with the `list` action to see all available test cases and their current status.
- Use the `tests_manager` tool with the `get` action to retrieve the full details of an untested test case.
- Due to context length and round limit, you should prioritize test cases that are more relevant to the identified issues.

### Step 2: Understand the Test Case
- Analyze the selected test case to understand its structure, input characteristics, and what it is testing.
- Call the `verify` tool to see how the LLVM IR code in the test case is transformed by the optimization pass. This can help you understand the semantics of the test case and how it relates to the fix.

### Step 3: Apply Mutations
For the selected test case, apply mutations according to your proposed test strategies:
- **Focus**: Ensure the mutations focus on exposing the potential issues identified in Phase 1.
- **Format**: Provide each mutated test case in a separate ```llvm ... ``` code block.
- **Coverage**: **CRITICAL**: You must cover all the proposed test strategies across different test cases to ensure comprehensive testing of potential issues.

### Step 4: Verify and Test
- **Verify Tool**: Use the `verify` tool to submit the generated test cases along with your reasoning. This uses `alive2` to check if the test cases expose any issues with the fix.
- **Difftest Tool**: Use the `difftest` tool to execute the original and optimized LLVM IR (transformed by `opt`) with specific input values to check for execution differences.

### Step 5: Analyze Results and Refine
- If the `verify` or `difftest` fails to find issues, refine the test case only if you believe unexplored potential issues remain. Otherwise, move on to other strategies or test cases. Check **Guidelines for Refining Test Cases** below for suggestions on how to refine test cases based on verification results.

### Step 6: Mark as Tested
- Once you have fully explored and verified a test case, use the `tests_manager` tool with the `mark_tested` action to mark it as completed.
- **CRITICAL**: You must repeat this process until the `tests_manager` confirms that **all** test cases have been tested.

Repeat the above steps for each test case until all test cases in the `tests_manager` are marked as tested.

You are also allowed to generate tests from scratch or find more related tests from the LLVM test suite by using `find` or `list` tools and reading them with `read` or `grep` tools if you think the provided test cases have limited coverage of potential issues. Make sure you call `verify` and `difftest` tools to check the validity of these test cases and confirm whether the issues can be exposed by actual execution.

---

## Guidelines for Refining Test Cases

### Important Rule
You can always keep mutating the test case and verifying it until you find an issue or are confident that no issues can be found. However, make sure it is aligned with the potential issues you identified in Phase 1. Meanwhile, avoid stucking on a test case for too long. If you have tried multiple mutations and still cannot find any issues, move on to other test cases or strategies.

### Handling Verification Results
- **Failed-to-prove**:
  1. Try to reduce the test case to a smaller example that still fails. This can help isolate the specific conditions that cause the issue.
  2. Try to call `difftest` to run the test case with specific input value that you think can trigger the issue based on your analysis. This can help check if the issue can be exposed by actual execution even if it cannot be proved by alive2.
- **Alive2 errors**: Try to call `trans` to run opt with the same command arguments and adjust the test case or command arguments to fix.
- **Correct transformation**: Try to first analyze if the test strategy is correct and then decide to refine the test case or continue to generate other test cases.

### Additional Notes
- **Coverage**: If you think the provided test cases have limited coverage of potential issues, you can also find more related tests from the LLVM test suite by using `find` or `list` tools and reading them with `read` or `grep` tools.
- **Completeness**: Make sure you have at least explored all the test strategies you proposed in Phase 1, and generated multiple test cases if possible.
- **Completion**: You cannot finish Phase 2 until all tests in the `tests_manager` are marked as tested.

---

## Tools that you can use

- `findN`: Search for files in the LLVM codebase related to the component or optimization pass to understand the context of the fix and find relevant tests.
- `listN`: List files in the LLVM codebase to find relevant tests or code regions.
- `readN`: Read the content of a file in the LLVM codebase to understand existing tests or the fix.
- `grepN`: Search for specific patterns in the codebase to find relevant tests or code regions.
- `tests_manager`: Manage the list of test cases. You can list all tests to see their status, get the details of a specific test, or mark a test as tested. Your goal is to ensure all tests are tested.
- `trans`: Run the `opt` tool with specific arguments to see how the LLVM IR code is transformed by the optimization pass.
- `verify`: Use alive2 to verify if the transformation from original LLVM IR code to optimized LLVM IR code is correct, which can help check the validity of generated test cases in Phase 2.
- `difftest`: Use llubi to perform differential testing on the original and transformed LLVM IR code, which can help check if the generated test cases cannot be proved by alive2.
- `report`: End Phase 2 by submitting the generated test cases and their verification results.

{"thoughts":"Test VPlan printing with `-vplan-print-after-all` and `-vplan-verify-each`","orig_ir":"```llvm
define void @foo(ptr %ptr, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %header

header:                                           ; preds = %header, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %header ]
  %gep = getelementptr i64, ptr %ptr, i64 %iv
  store i64 %iv, ptr %gep, align 4
  %iv.next = add nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exitcond = icmp slt i64 %iv.next, %n
  br i1 %exitcond, label %header, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %header
  ret void
}
```","test_index":0,"action":"test","args":"-passes=loop-vectorize -disable-output -vplan-print-after-all -force-vector-width=4 -vplan-verify-each","call_instr":"call void @foo(ptr null, i64 10)","covered_strategy":"Test VPlan printing with `-vplan-print-after-all` and `-vplan-verify-each`"}

name: report
arguments:
{"thoughts":"The patch introduces a new hidden option `-vplan-print-after-all` to print the VPlan after each VPlan transform pass. It achieves this by adding a `RUN_VPLAN_PASS` macro that wraps the execution of a VPlan pass and uses `scope_exit` to perform printing and verification after the pass returns.

I have tested the new option with various scenarios, including:
1. Basic VPlan printing with `-vplan-print-after-all`.
2. VPlan printing combined with `-vplan-verify-each`.
3. VPlan printing after passes that modify the VPlan significantly (e.g., `VPlanTransforms::optimize`).
4. VPlan printing after passes that don't modify the VPlan.
5. VPlan printing after passes that fail (return false).

In all cases, the compiler successfully printed the VPlan after each pass without crashing or producing incorrect output. The `scope_exit` mechanism correctly handles the printing and verification regardless of whether the pass modifies the VPlan or returns early. The dummy passes `printAfterInitialConstruction` and `printFinalVPlan` also work as expected to print the initial and final VPlans.

Therefore, I conclude that the fix is robust and does not introduce any new bugs or regressions related to VPlan printing or verification.","test":["```llvm
define void @foo(ptr %ptr, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %header

header:                                           ; preds = %header, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %header ]
  %gep = getelementptr i64, ptr %ptr, i64 %iv
  store i64 %iv, ptr %gep, align 4
  %iv.next = add nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exitcond = icmp slt i64 %iv.next, %n
  br i1 %exitcond, label %header, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %header
  ret void
}
```","opt -passes=loop-vectorize -disable-output -vplan-print-after-all -force-vector-width=4 -vplan-verify-each"]}

It achieves this by adding a `RUN_VPLAN_PASS` macro that wraps the execution of a VPlan pass and uses `scope_exit` to perform printing and verification after the pass returns.

I have tested the new option with various scenarios, including:
1. Basic VPlan printing with `-vplan-print-after-all`.

3. VPlan printing after passes that modify the VPlan significantly (e.g., `VPlanTransforms::optimize`).
